title: Bootstrap 1kEE desktop OSINT demo
description: Establish the initial Rust/egui desktop application skeleton, architecture docs, and first tracked slices for globe visualization, event ingestion, and camera feed integration.
notes: The globe renderer now consumes a real GEBCO-derived runtime height asset (Derived/terrain/gebco_2025_preview_4096.png) via a cached bilinear sampler. Procedural terrain remains only as fallback. Remaining terrain/rendering work is to consume actual contour vectors or tiled higher-resolution regional assets instead of just the preview raster.

title: Scaffold Rust workspace and egui desktop shell
description: Create the cargo workspace, desktop app crate, initial state model, and placeholder panels for map/event/camera interactions.
notes: Starting initial scaffold. Plan: create a Rust workspace with a desktop egui app, split modules by responsibility, and document the seams for future map, event, and camera integrations.
close_reason: Completed initial scaffold: Rust workspace, egui desktop shell, modular panels, seeded demo data model, companion docs, and build verification via cargo check.

title: Design globe and map interaction architecture
description: Define how the application will render a world map or globe, place events on geography, and query nearby cameras from selected locations.
notes: Started globe rendering work. Replacing the flat placeholder with a stylized dark globe while preserving event/camera selection semantics and modular docs.
close_reason: Completed the first globe slice: replaced the flat placeholder with an orthographic dark wireframe globe, added stylized continent contours and HUD overlays, preserved event/camera hit-testing, updated companion docs, and verified with cargo check.

title: Design event stream ingestion for Factal
description: Define the boundary for consuming curated Factal events, normalizing their location/time/priority data, and pushing them into the UI model for live visualization.

title: Design camera source registry and connection flow
description: Define how open camera metadata is stored, geocoded, filtered by proximity, and converted into attempted feed connections from the desktop app.

title: Integrate terrain asset discovery and preprocessing plan
description: Detect local GEBCO, Natural Earth, and SRTM assets from the Data directory, surface their status in the UI, and document the preprocessing pipeline for globe terrain/contour generation.
notes: Scanning local terrain datasets and wiring a manifest/status layer into the app. Also documenting the GDAL preprocessing path for GEBCO, Natural Earth, and the partially present SRTM mirror.
close_reason: Completed terrain asset discovery and preprocessing planning: detected local GEBCO, Natural Earth, and partial SRTM assets from Data/, surfaced terrain status in the UI model/header, documented the GDAL pipeline, and verified with cargo check.

title: Upgrade globe to interactive 3D camera with contour-driven LOD
description: Replace the static orthographic globe behavior with drag/scroll camera controls, zoom-based level of detail, and a contour-heavy 3D-first rendering language aligned with the target visual references.
notes: Implementing persistent globe camera state, drag/scroll interaction, zoom-dependent LOD, and a more contour-heavy 3D renderer. Also fixing terrain inventory discovery so the app can find Data/ from nested working directories.
close_reason: Completed the 3D globe interaction slice: added persistent camera state, drag orbit, wheel zoom, zoom-driven LOD, procedural contour-heavy globe rendering, and terrain discovery that works from nested working directories. Verified with cargo check.

title: Generate derived terrain assets from local datasets
description: Use the checked-in GEBCO and Natural Earth source rasters to build runtime-friendly derived assets under Derived/terrain, starting with a global VRT, preview raster, and contour outputs.
notes: Generating derived terrain assets under Derived/terrain from the local GEBCO and Natural Earth sources so the next globe pass can consume real preprocessed data instead of only procedural contours.
close_reason: Generated derived terrain assets from local GEBCO and Natural Earth sources: global VRT, 4096x2048 GEBCO preview raster, 500m and 200m contour GeoPackages, and Natural Earth relief preview. Validated outputs with gdalinfo/ogrinfo and documented the results.

title: Integrate derived GEBCO preview into globe renderer
description: Load the generated GEBCO preview raster as a runtime terrain asset and drive globe elevation/shading from real sampled terrain instead of the current procedural field.
notes: Integrated the derived GEBCO preview runtime asset into the globe renderer via a cached PNG sampler. The renderer now samples real terrain height when the runtime asset is present and falls back to the procedural field otherwise.
close_reason: Integrated the derived GEBCO preview runtime asset into the globe renderer: generated runtime PNG assets, added a cached terrain sampler, and switched globe elevation sampling from procedural-only to asset-backed with fallback. Verified with cargo check.

title: Add data-root picker and ingest derived contour vectors
description: Let the operator choose the project data root explicitly from the desktop UI and begin consuming derived GEBCO contour assets from that selected root for globe rendering.
notes: Implemented the next OSM ingest slice. osm_ingest.rs now has a manual global-road bootstrap queue, background worker tick, job snapshots, and a first pure-Rust import path that streams ways from planet-latest.osm.pbf into road_tile_manifest/road_tiles in Derived/osm/osm_runtime.sqlite. The worker is explicitly gated on the OSM header advertising LocationsOnWays so we do not attempt a full-planet ref-based road import that would explode memory. Terrain Library now surfaces an OSM section with Queue Global Roads, capability status, and job rows.

title: Fix globe rendering performance and zoom
